title: Developer Experience Without Overengineering
content: Developer experience improves when projects are predictable. Engineers should know where files live, how data flows, and how to run checks.
The easiest win is naming consistency. Components, utilities, and content folders should follow a clear convention from day one.
Avoid generic factories or abstraction layers without repeated use cases. They increase cognitive load and make onboarding harder.
Simple architecture scales surprisingly far when boundaries are explicit and code review standards stay high.
